beats.activity: Wake up, make coffee, check ferry schedule and weather.
beats.reasoning: Diego wakes early as always, obsessively checking the ferry schedule given the transit strike. Coffee ritual settles him before the day's logistical wrinkles.

beats.activity: Drive to Mercer Island ferry terminal and board ferry to Seattle.
beats.reasoning: With buses and light rail down until 4pm, ferry is the reliable option Diego knows by heart. Mild weather makes the ride pleasant.

beats.activity: Ferry crossing to downtown Seattle, watching the Sound.
beats.reasoning: Ferry ride is Diego's meditation; he uses the crossing time to mentally prepare for the day at Pike Place.

beats.activity: Walk from ferry terminal to Pike Place Market stall.
beats.reasoning: Short walk from ferry terminal to stall; Diego arrives early to prep flowers and check inventory before the market opens.

beats.activity: Work at florist stall: arranging bouquets, assisting customers, restocking flowers.
beats.reasoning: Morning and early afternoon at the stall. Partly cloudy weather is pleasant for the market; fewer tourists due to transit strike means a quieter day, which suits Diego's mood (energy 40/100 yesterday).

beats.activity: Lunch: grab usual sandwich from market vendor, eat in the back room.
beats.reasoning: Diego's routine lunch, same spot, same sandwich. Stays at the market to save time and money rather than venturing out during the transit strike.

beats.activity: Continue work at stall: afternoon rush, final restocking, closing prep.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift through closing time. Transit strike ends at 4pm, but Diego stays until normal closing. Quieter day allows time to organize stock and chat with regulars.

beats.activity: Walk from Pike Place Market to ferry terminal.
beats.reasoning: Transit strike has ended, but Diego prefers the ferry routine. Short walk back to terminal after a steady day at the stall.

beats.activity: Board ferry back to Mercer Island.
beats.reasoning: Evening ferry crossing; Diego reflects on the day and enjoys the quiet water before heading home.

beats.activity: Drive from ferry terminal to Safeway, pick up groceries for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Quick stop for salmon or fresh ingredients; Diego values his evening routine and prefers shopping on Mercer Island near home.

beats.activity: Arrive home, prepare simple dinner, pour a drink.
beats.reasoning: Diego cooks a modest dinner at home; this is his sanctuary time after a long day at the market.

beats.activity: Leisure: listen to vinyl records, browse video games, relax on the couch.
beats.reasoning: Evening wind-down with his passions—vinyl collecting and gaming. Low energy from yesterday carries over; Diego spends a quiet night at home recharging.
Diego heads to bed at a reasonable hour, getting a full night's sleep before Friday. The beat spans overnight as required.

diary:
Another ferry day. The Sound was glassy this morning, and I needed that calm before heading into the market. The transit strike had everyone talking, but it didn't really touch me—been taking the ferry for years anyway. Pike Place was busy, the usual Thursday energy. A young couple came in looking for something special, and I spent a good twenty minutes putting together this sunset-colored arrangement. Those moments still matter, even after all this time.

Grabbed my sandwich at noon, ate it standing in the back like always. The afternoon kept me on my feet, which is fine. Felt good to close up early and make the walk back to the terminal. By the time I got home with groceries, the light was that perfect golden thing. Put on some old Coltrane, poured a bourbon, and just sat with it. Nothing fancy. Just the day settling into evening.
thought_process: The strike didn't faze me—I've been insulated from that particular chaos by geography and routine. Ferry commuting feels almost meditative at this point, and honestly, watching the Sound this morning cleared my head in a way the news cycle never does. I stayed present with customers, especially that young couple; they reminded me why I still do this work. The afternoon energy was steady but nothing demanding. I chose to head home early partly because the crowds were manageable and partly because I could feel myself wanting quietness. The bourbon and vinyl felt earned, not indulgent. There's something about being fifty-seven, running a small stall, that makes you appreciate the shape of an ordinary day.
world_event_prompt: Mild, partly cloudy Thursday, high of 68F. Citywide transit strike: all Metro buses and Link Light Rail suspended until 4pm. Rideshare surge pricing in effect.
